title: dortmanna modesta
intro: Dortmanna modesta, commonly known as the Sea Spurge, is a plant species found in coastal areas. It is known for its medicinal properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation, due to its health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The therapeutic actions of Dortmanna modesta include wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents including saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids. These bioactive constituents work synergistically to provide the plant's therapeutic benefits, making it a valuable resource for natural medicine.
side_effects: Dortmanna modesta can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause stomach problems, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxins can lead to more severe health issues, including liver and kidney damage, as well as increased risk of cancer. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also cause skin irritation, including rashes and burns, in some individuals.
